key_points: Barracks living is spartan by design, not accidental
Rats have zero personal space or privacy during hell week
Rooms deliberately stripped of clocks to disorient new cadets
key_quotes: Days felt like weeks since you could never tell what time it was as a rat. The rooms didn't have clocks either, you were woken up by cadre.
Bring as little as possible.
Everything you need during hell week will be provided as long as you have the things on the list.

key_points: Brother Rat bond is the defining social relationship at VMI
Student athletes miss significant portions of the rat experience
Social life is insular — Lexington is small and options are limited
key_quotes: Student athletes miss out on a solid chunk of the rat experience.
I would burst out laughing every time — I'm sure they must have some other worldly inside jokes.
A robust but closeted sense of humor about it all.

key_points: No specific dining data available from student sources
Physical demands mean eating a lot and eating healthy is critical
Student athletes specifically advised to 'eat a lot and eat healthy food'
key_quotes: Eat a lot and eat healthy food.
Everything you need during hell week will be provided.
Bring a first aid kit and LOTS OF MOLE SKIN.

key_points: Psychological strain of rat line is intentional and sustained over seven months
Physical toll is severe — hands destroyed by cleaning chemicals, blisters everywhere
Mental toughness is the product, not a byproduct — some students genuinely need this structure
key_quotes: Your hands will get demolished by brasso and other cleaners, a tub of Vaseline will help. Bring a first aid kit and LOTS OF MOLE SKIN. Parts of your body you never knew exist will blister.
Keep your sense of humor and laugh on the inside.
I have great respect for the cadet self aware enough to realize he needed strict discipline.

key_points: Tuition runs approximately $60,000 per year per outside observers
No VMI-specific financial aid data surfaced in student comments
ROTC contracts and military scholarships are common pathways to affordability
key_quotes: Imagine going here and paying $60k when you can join the real military instead and they'll pay for all your schooling.
I owe VMI everything. Commissioned and left after my first contract. Joined the civilian world and made $185,000 total comp my first year as a 28 year old.
Paid tuition the whole time and went non-com route after a year and a half because of a medical issue.

key_points: ECE and STEM majors face intense workload on top of military obligations
Psych major is mocked internally — STEM and pre-commission tracks are the core identity
VMI graduates praised by military peers as academically and mentally prepared
key_quotes: Psych major is the cherry on top for me.
I graduated, commissioned but got out early to pursue Ph.D in computer science. I've been at the same company as a principal computer scientist supporting the Army for over 23 years.
This VMI graduate was an extreme hard charger, mentally tough, physically tough, and always motivated to do the hardest jobs.

key_points: New superintendent Furness expected to crack down on lax standards post-COVID
Standards visibly slipped — out-of-regs haircuts and uniforms noted publicly
Political pressure around defunding and female cadet policies creating institutional instability
key_quotes: A lot of issues have been festering since Covid.
Keep the shitbags in your prayers because Furness is about to flip their worlds upside down.
It looks like the bill to create a task force to defund VMI passed the rules committee 4 to 1 on a party line vote.

key_points: Lexington Virginia is extremely small — graduation restaurant reservations are nearly impossible
Natural Bridge area is rural with very limited off-campus options
Campus itself is historic and visually striking but geographically isolated
key_quotes: You will need a lot of luck for something like that during VMI graduation booking at this late time.
You could take a look at Staunton — they've got some places that could take reservations and support a group that large.
VMI is an otherworldly place, God bless it.

key_points: Alumni report dramatic civilian salary jumps after short military service
Non-commission route still yields strong outcomes — discipline transfers across industries
VMI grads trusted and respected by peers from other military institutions
key_quotes: I owe VMI everything. Made $185,000 total comp my first year as a 28 year old. Moved up the ranks and now earn over $500,000.
Whenever I meet a fellow graduate from the Naval Academy, Norwich, Texas A&M, or VMI, I know I can trust them.
VMI is a different story… said with clear implication of elite respect by a Naval Academy Marine officer.

key_points: $60K tuition with zero military pay is the core financial tension
ROI is strong long-term but only if you commission or leverage the discipline in civilian career
Many observers question cost versus just enlisting and getting education paid
key_quotes: Imagine going here and paying $60k when you can join the real military instead and they'll pay for all your schooling.
I owe VMI everything. Commissioned (total comp was $50k) and left after my first contract. Joined the civilian world and made $185,000 total comp my first year.
ROTC+. Imagine explaining this during the holidays: 'So you're in the military?' 'No… I just like getting called a dirty little rat.'

key_points: Cult-like loyalty among alumni — nearly universal 'I'd do it again' sentiment
Current cadets display dark humor as core coping mechanism
Outside observers oscillate between respect and bewilderment at the model
key_quotes: VMI is an otherworldly place, God bless it.
A guy that went here studied at my uni for a semester. At times I thought he was exaggerating his stories but no, I was wrong. Goddamn.
It kills a lot of bad habits and laziness that a regular college will let you get away with.

key_points: **Hazing History:** Uncle broke his wrist in stairwell tradition involving upperclassmen beatings
**Standards Erosion:** Out-of-regs haircuts and uniform violations openly mocked on r/vmi post-COVID
**Political Instability:** Defunding task force passed committee 4-1; alumni discussing going private
key_quotes: He said he broke his wrist on his way down.
Haircut out of regs (pretty common on post these days), mustache, collar is fucked.
The bill to create a task force to defund VMI passed the rules committee 4 to 1 on a party line vote.

key_points: Brother Rat system creates lifelong bonds unlike anything at civilian universities
Rat line time deprivation is a deliberate psychological tool — no watches, no clocks, no control
VMI alumni are a trusted network — grads from peer institutions say they immediately trust VMI people
key_quotes: None of the rats are wearing watches or any time telling device but all the cadre are. This is very much intentional and deeply affected my hell week experience.
Whenever I meet a fellow graduate from VMI, I know I can trust them.

Back in Marine Corps OCS we had a VMI graduate in my platoon. This VMI graduate was an extreme hard charger — now he is a Marine Corps infantry officer leading his Marines with discipline and motivation.
